(Jimmy of the Clue Crew reports from an urban Asian street.) I'm in the old quarter of this capital city that once had the name Tonkin

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Sarah of the Clue Crew reports from a beach with tropical blue waters.) Behind me is this Pacific island whose double-talk name means "first born"; according to Polynesian mythology, it's the first island that the god Ta'aroa fished out of the water

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Kelly of the Clue Crew reports from a hillside with architecture behind her.) I'm at the building housing the president's office in this administrative, not legislative or judicial, capital of South Africa

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Sarah of the Clue Crew reports from up high, with the sea behind her.) Named for the sailor who first sighted it in 1767, I'm on this island that was eventually populated by the mutineers of the Bounty

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Kelly of the Clue Crew reports, flanked by soldiers in fatigues and with a helicopter behind her.) I'm at the U.S. Army garrison in this German city that was once a spa town where Goethe, Brahms & Dostoevsky all took the waters; the "baden" in its name means "baths"
